title: SPEC-043 Addon Lifecycle Events — Gaps Remediation

tasks.title: Add clearEntitlementCache after cancelAddon in route
tasks.description:
Add `clearEntitlementCache(customerId)` call in the cancelUserAddon route handler immediately after `cancelAddon()` returns successfully.

Files affected:
- `apps/api/src/routes/billing/addons.ts` — locate the DELETE /addons/:id or equivalent cancel handler, add cache clear call after the service call

Acceptance criteria:
- `clearEntitlementCache(customerId)` is called in the route handler after successful cancellation
- The call is fire-and-forget (no await needed for the route response)
- Unit test verifies cache clear is called on success and NOT called on error

Test file: `apps/api/test/routes/billing/addons.test.ts` (add assertions).

subtasks.title: Locate cancel route handler in addons.ts
Add clearEntitlementCache call after cancelAddon()
Add/update test assertions for cache clear

tasks.title: Add clearEntitlementCache after confirmAddonPurchase insert
tasks.description:
Add `clearEntitlementCache(customerId)` in `confirmAddonPurchase()` after the successful DB insert of the addon purchase.

Files affected:
- `apps/api/src/services/addon.checkout.ts` — in `confirmAddonPurchase()`, after the insert completes, call `clearEntitlementCache(customerId)`

Acceptance criteria:
- Cache is cleared after every successful addon confirmation
- Cache is NOT cleared if the insert fails or is rolled back
- Test asserts cache clear is invoked with correct customerId

subtasks.title: Locate confirmAddonPurchase insert in addon.checkout.ts
Add clearEntitlementCache call post-insert
Verify test covers cache-clear-on-success path

tasks.title: Add subscription status re-check guard in confirmAddonPurchase
tasks.description:
Before confirming addon purchase, re-verify that the subscription status is not 'cancelled'. This prevents a race condition where the subscription is cancelled between checkout creation and purchase confirmation.

Files affected:
- `apps/api/src/services/addon.checkout.ts` — add guard at the start of `confirmAddonPurchase()`: fetch subscription, check `status !== 'cancelled'`, return error if cancelled

Acceptance criteria:
- If subscription is cancelled at confirm time, return a typed error (not throw)
- If subscription is active/trialing, proceed normally
- Test covers: active subscription proceeds, cancelled subscription returns error, edge case where subscription not found

Test file: `apps/api/test/services/addon.checkout.test.ts`.

subtasks.title: Add subscription re-fetch at start of confirmAddonPurchase
Add status guard: return error if status === 'cancelled'
Add tests for cancelled and active subscription paths

tasks.title: Fix catch-all customer existence check in webhook subscription-logic
tasks.description:
In `subscription-logic.ts` lines 424-450, the current catch-all treats all errors as 'customer not found'. Discriminate between 404 (not found) and other errors (propagate as 500).

Files affected:
- `apps/api/src/routes/webhooks/mercadopago/subscription-logic.ts` — update catch block to check error type/status code; only treat HTTP 404 or specific not-found error as missing customer; re-throw or return 500 for other error types

Acceptance criteria:
- 404/not-found error: log warning, return 200 (customer not found is non-fatal)
- Other errors (network, DB, 5xx): propagate as 500 or log as error
- Test: mock lookup throwing 404 vs 503 and verify different handling paths

Linked: GAP-043-27 test covers the not-found vs transient error distinction.

subtasks.title: Identify current catch-all block in subscription-logic.ts:424-450
Add error type discrimination (404 vs other)
Update test for 404 and 503 handling paths

tasks.title: Wrap cancelUserAddon DB update + recalculation in transaction
tasks.description:
Add database transaction wrapping the status update and limit recalculation in `cancelUserAddon()`. If recalculation fails, the transaction rolls back the cancellation. Also add Sentry capture on recalc failure.

Files affected:
- `apps/api/src/services/addon.user-addons.ts` — wrap `db.update(status=cancelled)` + `recalculateLimits()` in a `db.transaction()` block; if recalc throws, rollback and capture in Sentry

Acceptance criteria:
- DB update + recalc are atomic: both succeed or both rollback
- `Sentry.captureException()` called on recalc failure
- Test: mock recalc to throw, verify DB status NOT changed (rollback verified)
- Test: happy path, verify both update and recalc complete

Dependency: T-001 (cache clear should happen after the transaction commits, not inside it).

subtasks.title: Wrap status update + recalc in db.transaction()
Add Sentry.captureException on recalc failure
Test rollback path: recalc throws, verify status unchanged
Test happy path: both operations complete atomically

tasks.title: Add billingLoadFailed flag and 503 response to entitlement middleware
tasks.description:
Add a `billingLoadFailed` boolean flag to the entitlement context. When billing data fails to load, set this flag. The `requireLimit` middleware must return 503 Service Unavailable when `billingLoadFailed` is true, rather than allowing or denying access silently.

Files affected:
- `apps/api/src/middlewares/entitlement.ts` — add `billingLoadFailed` to context type; set it on billing load failure; update `requireLimit` to check flag and return 503

Acceptance criteria:
- Entitlement context type includes `billingLoadFailed: boolean`
- On billing service failure, context has `billingLoadFailed: true`
- `requireLimit` returns 503 with appropriate error body when `billingLoadFailed` is true
- Test: billing service throws -> middleware returns 503
- Test: billing succeeds -> normal flow

Test file: `apps/api/test/middlewares/entitlement.test.ts`.

subtasks.title: Add billingLoadFailed to entitlement context type
Set billingLoadFailed=true on billing service failure
Add 503 response in requireLimit when billingLoadFailed
Add tests for 503 path and normal path

tasks.title: Add ADDON_EXPIRED notification dispatch in expiry cron
tasks.description:
In the addon expiry cron job loop, add `sendNotification()` with `ADDON_EXPIRED` type after marking each addon as expired. Use an idempotency key to prevent duplicate notifications.

Files affected:
- `apps/api/src/cron/jobs/addon-expiry.job.ts` — after expiry status update in loop, call `sendNotification({ type: 'ADDON_EXPIRED', customerId, payload: {...}, idempotencyKey: 'addon_expired:${customerId}:${addonSlug}:${YYYY-MM-DD}' })`

Acceptance criteria:
- `sendNotification` is called for each expired addon in the cron loop
- Idempotency key format: `addon_expired:{customerId}:{addonSlug}:{YYYY-MM-DD}`
- Notification failure does not stop the cron loop (fire-and-forget with try-catch)
- Test: cron runs, verify sendNotification called once per expired addon
- Test: duplicate run same day, verify idempotency key prevents double send

Test file: `apps/api/test/cron/addon-expiry.test.ts`.

subtasks.title: Add sendNotification call in expiry loop
Define idempotency key format with date suffix
Wrap in try-catch so notification failure doesn't break cron
Add tests for notification dispatch and idempotency

tasks.title: Add Promise.race 20s timeout for webhook addon lifecycle calls
tasks.description:
Add a `Promise.race` with a 20-second timeout around the addon lifecycle calls in the webhook handler. If the timeout fires first, return HTTP 200 immediately so MercadoPago does not retry, and let the cron job complete processing.

Files affected:
- `apps/api/src/routes/webhooks/mercadopago/subscription-logic.ts` — wrap the main lifecycle service call in `Promise.race([lifecycleCall, timeout(20_000)])`; on timeout, log warning and return 200 without error

Acceptance criteria:
- Lifecycle calls that complete within 20s proceed normally
- Calls exceeding 20s: return 200 to MercadoPago, log a warning, do NOT throw
- The underlying lifecycle operation may still be running (fire-and-forget after timeout)
- Test: mock lifecycle to delay 25s (fake timers), verify 200 returned before lifecycle completes
- Test: mock lifecycle to complete in 5s, verify normal flow

Test file: `apps/api/test/webhooks/mercadopago.test.ts`.

subtasks.title: Create timeout() helper or inline Promise.race pattern
Wrap lifecycle call in Promise.race with 20s timeout
On timeout: log warning, return 200
Test timeout path with fake timers
Test normal path completes under timeout

tasks.title: Add concurrent webhook + admin cancellation integration tests
tasks.description:
Add integration tests using `Promise.all()` to simulate concurrent cancellation from both webhook and admin routes. Verify idempotency: exactly one succeeds, the other detects already-cancelled state. Verify no double-revocation of entitlements.

Files affected:
- `apps/api/test/integration/` — new test file `addon-lifecycle-concurrency.test.ts`

Test scenarios:
1. Two concurrent cancellation calls: verify exactly one returns success, the other returns already-cancelled (idempotent)
2. Concurrent webhook + admin cancel: no double entitlement revocation
3. Concurrent cancel + plan change: verify deterministic outcome

Acceptance criteria:
- All 3 scenarios implemented with Promise.all()
- No flakiness (use proper test isolation and locks)
- Tests run in vitest integration suite

subtasks.title: Create addon-lifecycle-concurrency.test.ts
Test scenario 1: two concurrent cancellations
Test scenario 2: webhook + admin cancel, no double revocation
Test scenario 3: cancel + plan change concurrency

tasks.title: Add partial failure retry tests for addon lifecycle cancellation
tasks.description:
Add tests verifying partial failure behavior: 3 addons processed, #2 fails. Verify #1 is cancelled, #2 and #3 remain active. Then simulate retry: verify #1 is skipped (already cancelled), #2 is retried and processed, #3 is processed.

Files affected:
- `apps/api/test/services/addon-lifecycle-cancellation.service.test.ts` — add new describe block for partial failure scenarios

Test scenarios:
1. Partial failure: 3 addons, #2 throws -> #1 cancelled, #2/#3 active
2. Retry after partial failure: #1 skipped (idempotent), #2 retried OK, #3 processed OK
3. All fail: verify rollback state is clean

Acceptance criteria:
- Tests use mocked DB with selective failure injection
- Retry logic is verified to be idempotent (double-cancel = no-op)
- Test assertions check exact status of each addon after each run

subtasks.title: Add partial failure test: 3 addons, #2 fails

tasks.title: Add cache race condition tests for entitlement cache
tasks.description:
Add tests covering concurrent cache read + clear race conditions. Verify behavior when a read happens between the DB update and the cache clear.

Files affected:
- New or existing test file: `apps/api/test/services/addon-entitlement.service.test.ts` or similar — add describe block 'cache race conditions'

Test scenarios:
1. Concurrent read during cache clear: verify eventual consistency (stale data returned once, fresh on retry)
2. Multiple cache clears for same customer: verify idempotency
3. Cache populated from DB when empty: verify no race between populate and clear
4. Parallel requests race: two parallel reads, verify no corrupted state

Acceptance criteria:
- Uses fake timers or explicit Promise sequencing to simulate race
- At least 4 race condition scenarios covered
- Tests are deterministic (no flakiness)

Depends on T-007 (transaction work stabilizes the sequence).

subtasks.title: Test: concurrent read during clear -> stale once, fresh on retry
Test: multiple clears for same customer -> idempotent
Test: cache populate vs clear race
Test: parallel requests, no corrupted state

tasks.title: Add graceful degradation tests for billing service failure
tasks.description:
Add 4 tests covering graceful degradation when billing service fails:
1. Billing fails -> entitlement context uses plan entitlements only (no addons)
2. Billing fails -> no addon entitlements in context
3. Billing fails -> shouldCache=false (don't cache failure state)
4. Billing fails -> request succeeds (degraded mode), retry works on next request

Files affected:
- `apps/api/test/middlewares/entitlement.test.ts` — add 'graceful degradation' describe block

Acceptance criteria:
- All 4 scenarios implemented
- Uses mocked billing service that throws
- Verifies context shape for degraded mode
- Verifies shouldCache=false on failure

Depends on T-008 (billingLoadFailed flag implementation).

subtasks.title: Test 1: billing fails -> plan entitlements only
Test 2: billing fails -> no addon entitlements
Test 3: billing fails -> shouldCache=false
Test 4: degraded request succeeds, retry works

tasks.title: Add retry backoff and error classification in cron
tasks.description:
Add exponential backoff to the cron retry logic: skip retry if `lastAttempt + (retryCount * 2 days) > now()`. Classify errors into retryable (network, transient DB) vs non-retryable (invalid state, not found).

Files affected:
- `apps/api/src/cron/jobs/addon-expiry.job.ts` — add backoff calculation before retry; add error type classification
- `apps/api/src/services/addon-expiration.service.ts` — add error classification helper or inline

Acceptance criteria:
- Backoff formula: skip if `lastAttempt + (retryCount * 2 days) > now()`
- Retryable: network errors, 5xx, DB connection errors
- Non-retryable: 404, invalid status transitions, already expired
- Test: retryCount=1 -> 2-day window; retryCount=2 -> 4-day window
- Test: non-retryable error -> marked as failed, no further retries

subtasks.title: Add error classification (retryable vs non-retryable)
Implement backoff: skip if lastAttempt + retryCount*2days > now()
Test: retryCount-based window enforcement
Test: non-retryable error stops retries

tasks.title: Add advisory lock per customer in limit recalculation
tasks.description:
Add `pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext(customerId))` during limit recalculation to prevent concurrent recalculations for the same customer corrupting the limits.

Files affected:
- `apps/api/src/services/addon-limit-recalculation.service.ts` — at the start of the recalculation transaction, call `SELECT pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext('${customerId}'))` via Drizzle raw query

Acceptance criteria:
- Lock acquired inside the transaction (pg_advisory_xact_lock, not pg_try — this one blocks until lock available)
- Lock auto-released when transaction commits or rolls back
- Test: two concurrent recalcs for same customer -> second waits, then proceeds; no data corruption
- Note: uses xact_lock (not try_) because we want to block, not skip

subtasks.title: Add pg_advisory_xact_lock in recalculation transaction
Verify lock is inside the existing transaction
Test: concurrent recalculations serialize correctly

tasks.title: Add expect.objectContaining assertions in critical test paths
tasks.description:
Add `expect.objectContaining()` assertions (or `toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.objectContaining(...))`) in critical test paths where mocked functions are called with complex objects.

Files affected:
- `apps/api/test/services/addon-lifecycle.service.test.ts`
- `apps/api/test/services/addon-lifecycle-cancellation.service.test.ts`
- `apps/api/test/routes/billing/admin/subscription-cancel.test.ts`

Acceptance criteria:
- At least 10 test assertions upgraded from `toHaveBeenCalled()` to `toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.objectContaining(...))`
- All upgraded assertions verify the most critical fields
- No test coverage regression

subtasks.title: Identify tests with bare toHaveBeenCalled() in lifecycle tests
Upgrade 10+ assertions to toHaveBeenCalledWith(objectContaining)
Verify no test regressions

tasks.title: Return HTTP 409 for concurrent admin subscription cancel
tasks.description:
In the subscription cancel route, return HTTP 409 Conflict if the subscription is already cancelled when the admin cancel request arrives.

Files affected:
- `apps/api/src/routes/billing/admin/subscription-cancel.ts` — in the cancel handler, after fetching subscription, check if already cancelled; return 409 with message 'Subscription already cancelled'

Acceptance criteria:
- Already-cancelled subscription -> 409 response
- Response body: `{ error: 'ALREADY_CANCELLED', message: 'Subscription already cancelled' }`
- Test: cancel already-cancelled subscription -> 409
- Test: cancel active subscription -> 200

subtasks.title: Add already-cancelled check in cancel handler
Return 409 with ALREADY_CANCELLED error body
Test: already cancelled -> 409; active -> 200

tasks.title: Add TRIALING status to webhook safety net condition
tasks.description:
Expand the webhook safety net condition to include `TRIALING` status in addition to the currently handled statuses.

Files affected:
- `apps/api/src/routes/webhooks/mercadopago/subscription-logic.ts` — locate the safety net condition check; add `|| status === 'trialing'` to the condition

Acceptance criteria:
- TRIALING subscriptions handled by safety net the same as other active-adjacent statuses
- Test: webhook with TRIALING subscription -> safety net triggers

subtasks.title: Add TRIALING to safety net condition in subscription-logic.ts
Test: TRIALING webhook -> safety net triggers

tasks.title: Move promo code usage recording to confirmAddonPurchase
tasks.description:
Move promo code usage recording from `createAddonCheckout` to `confirmAddonPurchase` to prevent recording usage for checkouts that are never confirmed.

Files affected:
- `apps/api/src/services/addon.checkout.ts` — remove promo code usage recording from `createAddonCheckout`; add it to `confirmAddonPurchase` after successful insert

Acceptance criteria:
- Promo code usage NOT recorded at checkout creation
- Promo code usage recorded at purchase confirmation
- Abandoned checkouts do not consume promo code uses
- Test: checkout created but not confirmed -> usage count unchanged
- Test: checkout confirmed -> usage count incremented

subtasks.title: Remove promo code usage recording from createAddonCheckout
Add promo code usage recording to confirmAddonPurchase
Test: abandoned checkout -> usage unchanged
Test: confirmed purchase -> usage incremented

tasks.title: Wrap fire-and-forget sendNotification calls in try-catch
tasks.description:
Wrap all fire-and-forget `sendNotification` calls in try-catch blocks to prevent unhandled promise rejections from crashing the main flow.

Files affected:
- Any file calling `sendNotification` without await and without try-catch — audit all call sites and add try-catch wrapper
- Common locations: `apps/api/src/cron/jobs/addon-expiry.job.ts`, `apps/api/src/services/addon.user-addons.ts`, `apps/api/src/services/addon.checkout.ts`

Acceptance criteria:
- All fire-and-forget sendNotification calls wrapped in try-catch
- Caught errors logged at warn level (not error, since it's non-critical)
- Test: sendNotification throws -> main flow continues, warn logged

subtasks.title: Audit all sendNotification call sites for missing try-catch
Add try-catch with warn log to each call site
Test: notification throws -> flow continues

tasks.title: Add idempotency key to downgrade limit warning notification
tasks.description:
Add an idempotency key to the downgrade limit warning notification to prevent duplicate sends.

Files affected:
- The code dispatching the downgrade limit warning notification (likely in `addon-plan-change.service.ts` or `addon-downgrade-detection.service.ts`) — add `idempotencyKey: 'downgrade_warning:${customerId}:${limitKey}:${YYYY-MM-DD}'`

Acceptance criteria:
- Idempotency key format: `downgrade_warning:{customerId}:{limitKey}:{YYYY-MM-DD}`
- Duplicate dispatch on same day for same customer+limitKey -> deduplicated by notification service
- Test: two dispatches with same key same day -> only one notification sent

subtasks.title: Add idempotencyKey to downgrade warning dispatch call
Test: same key same day -> deduplicated

tasks.title: Add Phase 5 entitlement reconciliation to cron job
tasks.description:
Add a Phase 5 to the addon expiry cron job that queries for purchases with `entitlementRemovalPending=true` and retries the entitlement removal.

Files affected:
- `apps/api/src/cron/jobs/addon-expiry.job.ts` — add Phase 5: query `billing_addon_purchases WHERE entitlementRemovalPending = true`; retry `removeEntitlement(purchaseId)` for each; clear flag on success

Acceptance criteria:
- Phase 5 runs after Phases 1-4
- Queries purchases with pending entitlement removal
- Retries removal, clears flag on success
- Logs each retry attempt
- Test: purchase with entitlementRemovalPending=true -> Phase 5 retries and clears flag

subtasks.title: Add entitlementRemovalPending query in Phase 5
Retry removeEntitlement for each pending purchase
Clear entitlementRemovalPending flag on success
Add logging for each retry attempt
Test: pending flag -> retried and cleared

tasks.title: Split addon-expiration.service.ts into queries and batch processing files
tasks.description:
Refactor the oversized `addon-expiration.service.ts` by extracting DB queries to a separate file and batch processing logic to another.

Files affected:
- `apps/api/src/services/addon-expiration.queries.ts` (new) — extract all Drizzle queries related to expiration
- `apps/api/src/services/addon-expiration.batch.ts` (new) — extract batch processing loop and batch-level error handling
- `apps/api/src/services/addon-expiration.service.ts` — keep service orchestration only; import from new files

Acceptance criteria:
- addon-expiration.service.ts is under 500 lines
- Each new file is under 500 lines
- No circular imports
- All existing tests pass unchanged
- Named exports only

subtasks.title: Extract DB queries to addon-expiration.queries.ts
Extract batch loop to addon-expiration.batch.ts
Update imports in addon-expiration.service.ts
Verify all tests pass post-refactor
Verify all files under 500 lines
